changes_made: Renamed k8s_cluster_uses_supported_version to k8s_cluster_supported_version for brevity and consistency
review_notes: The functions are applicable to Kubernetes as they address maintaining a secure and compliant environment by ensuring supported versions, patching compliance, and vulnerability scanning. The naming conventions mostly follow the pattern but needed a minor adjustment for consistency. The functions are technically accurate and map well to Kubernetes security practices.
naming_improvements: Simplified the function name for cluster version check to align with naming best practices
title: and support customized approach for Requirements 6
step1_reasoning: The control 6.2.4 involves ensuring that systems are kept up-to-date and compliant with security patches and supported versions. In Kubernetes, this translates to ensuring that the cluster is running a supported version, nodes and pods are compliant with patching policies, and images are scanned for vulnerabilities. These checks align with maintaining a secure and compliant Kubernetes environment.
